---
title: "Research"
pagetitle: "Research · Arpan Acharya"
---

My research sits at the intersection of **applied econometrics**,
**development economics**, **political economy**, and **health economics**,
with most of my empirical work grounded in the South Asian — and particularly
the Nepali — context.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Some of the work below is ongoing or in draft form. If you'd like a copy of a
manuscript, please [get in touch](mailto:arpanacharya11.aa@gmail.com).
:::

## Research positions

- **Research Consultant**, Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA) — *2026 – present*
- **Research Associate**, HERD International — *2025 – 2026*
- **Research Assistant**, Department of Economics, Kathmandu University — *2024 – 2025* (Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari)
- **Policy Research Fellow & Researcher**, Samriddhi Foundation — *2024*
- **Freelance Research Assistant**, independent / remote — *2023 – present*

## Work in progress

### Allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal's health sector {.research-item}
*Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA) — ongoing*

Benchmarks efficiency across providers and units in Nepal's health system,
drawing on provider-level and national survey data. The goal is evidence that
can feed into health-financing and resource-allocation decisions in Nepal.

### Gender norms, fertility, and female labour force participation {.research-item}
*With Dr. Santosh Adhikari — ongoing*

An independent research project on how social norms around gender and
fertility shape women's participation in the labour force in Nepal. Work on
this began during my time as a Research Assistant at Kathmandu University and
continues in collaboration with Dr. Santosh Adhikari.

## Work-related research

### Econometric modelling of sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) taxation {.research-item}
*HERD International — 2025 – 2026*

Contributed to modelling the economic and health effects of **taxing
sugar-sweetened beverages** in Nepal. The work combined household expenditure
data with price and consumption data to study demand responses, tax
pass-through, and distributional consequences across different tax designs.

### State Marginalization and the Onset of Maoist Insurgency in Nepal {.research-item}
*Samriddhi Foundation — 2024.* Supervisor: Ms. Arpita Nepal

A research manuscript on the political-economy roots of Nepal's Maoist
insurgency, with a focus on how patterns of state marginalization shaped
conflict onset.

### Local Governance Index for Madhesh Province {.research-item}
*Samriddhi Foundation — 2024*

Designed and built a **Local Governance Index** for Madhesh Province to
benchmark governance performance across local units, alongside broader work on
state marginalization and inclusion.

## Undergraduate research works

### The Caste-Based Wage Gap in Nepal: Extent, Trends, and Explanations {.research-item}
*Undergraduate thesis, Kathmandu University — 2024 (work in progress).* Supervisor: Dr. Mani Nepal

Uses nationally representative Nepali labour-market data to document the size
and evolution of wage gaps between caste groups and decompose how much of the
gap is accounted for by observable characteristics versus unexplained
components.

### Impact of Reservation Policy on the Education of Caste-Disadvantaged People in Nepal {.research-item}
*Applied Economic Research course, Kathmandu University — 2023.* Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari

Examines how reservation-based affirmative action policies have shaped
educational attainment among historically disadvantaged caste groups in Nepal.

### Balancing Growth and Sustainability: Carbon Emissions, Energy Consumption, and Economic Growth in South Asia {.research-item}
*Presented at the 19th South Asian Economics Students' Meet — 2024*

A cross-country empirical study of the relationship between carbon emissions,
energy consumption, and economic growth across South Asia.

### Decoding Election 2022: Unveiling the Layer of Electoral Success {.research-item}
*Empirical brief, with Subin Aryal — 2023*

A short empirical brief analysing correlates of electoral success in Nepal's
2022 general elections.

## Conference presentations

- **19th South Asian Economics Students' Meet**, 2024
- **Tribhuvan University CAICE International Conference**, 2023 and 2024
- **Harmony & Hues**, interdepartmental conference, Kathmandu University
